intro: C++ requires manual memory management, explicit type declarations, and compile-time resolution — Python automates all of that. Porting C++ to Python means replacing `new`/`delete` with Python's garbage collector, translating pointer arithmetic to list indexing, converting C++ templates to Python generics or duck-typed functions, and replacing `std::vector`/`std::map` with `list`/`dict`. Header files disappear entirely.

diffs.from: Manual new/delete or RAII smart pointers. Destructors run deterministically.
diffs.to: Garbage collected. Reference counting via CPython. No delete.

diffs.from: Static, strong. Every variable has a compile-time type.
diffs.to: Dynamic, duck-typed. Type hints optional. No compile step.

diffs.from: Compiled to native machine code. Headers + source files.
diffs.to: Interpreted. Single file per module. No headers.
list, dict, set built-in. Comprehensions for inline creation.

diffs.from: try/catch with exception types; error codes common in C-style APIs.
diffs.to: Exceptions only. No error codes. try/except at the boundary.

examples.title: Vector operations
examples.description: C++ std::vector maps directly to Python list. Iterator loops become comprehensions.
examples.toCode:
nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]
evens = [n for n in nums if n % 2 == 0]
print(*evens)

examples.title: Class with constructor and method
examples.description: C++ class with explicit constructor and destructor translates to a Python class — GC handles cleanup.

tips.title: Delete memory management
tips.body: Remove all new, delete, and smart pointer (unique_ptr, shared_ptr) declarations. Python's garbage collector handles deallocation automatically.

tips.title: Remove header files
tips.body: C++ separates declaration (.h) from implementation (.cpp). Python has none of that — everything lives in a single .py file. Merge headers into the module.

tips.title: std::vector → list, std::map → dict
tips.body: Replace all STL containers with Python built-ins. vector<T> → list, map<K,V> → dict, unordered_set<T> → set, pair<A,B> → tuple.

tips.title: Remove const and & from signatures
tips.body: C++ const T& parameters become plain Python parameters. Python passes objects by reference automatically — no & needed.
